The founding story of StepUp Analytics — and why “customer first” became more than a slogan
Press enter or click to view image in full size

www.stepupanalytics.comA few years ago, if you’d told me I’d spend my days thinking about dentist appointment forms, WhatsApp emergency lines, and Google Business Profile duplicates, I would have laughed. I came from data science — R scripts, statistical models, the kind of work where the “customer” was usually a dashboard, not a person sitting in a waiting room.
StepUp Analytics didn’t start as a healthcare-focused web agency. It started as exactly what the name suggests: analytics. Data science, statistical modeling, the occasional R package. It was technical, satisfying work — and it taught me almost nothing about what it actually feels like to run a small business that depends on people walking through your door.
That gap is where the real story begins.
The pivot nobody plans for
Somewhere along the way, working with small businesses in Amsterdam, a pattern kept showing up. It wasn’t a data problem. It was a visibility problem, a trust problem, and — more often than I expected — a basic technology problem.
A local business would have a website that looked fine on the surface but was quietly invisible to Google because of a JavaScript rendering issue nobody had caught. A clinic’s Google Business Profile would have a duplicate listing splitting its reviews and phone numbers, tanking local search rankings without anyone noticing why. A practice would be relying on a five-year-old website that hadn’t been touched since launch, still listing services they no longer offered.
None of this needed a statistical model. It needed someone willing to sit with the business, understand exactly how patients or clients actually found them, and fix the unglamorous stuff first.
That’s the moment StepUp Analytics quietly turned into something else: an advanced web development and design firm, still carrying the analytical instincts of its data science roots, but now applied to helping small businesses actually grow digitally — end to end.
Why healthcare, specifically
Healthcare wasn’t a market I chose from a spreadsheet. It found me, largely because dental and physiotherapy practices in Amsterdam kept running into the exact same wall: they were excellent at patient care and completely underserved on the digital side.
These are businesses where trust is everything. A prospective patient searching “tandarts amsterdam” or “spoedtandarts” at 11pm because of a dental emergency isn’t casually browsing — they need an answer now, and they need to trust what they find enough to book immediately. If a clinic’s site loads slowly, buries its emergency contact info, or doesn’t show up in local search at all, that patient goes to a competitor. Every time.
Working end to end with dental and physiotherapy clients taught me that “digital transformation” in healthcare isn’t a single fix — it’s a chain of trust signals that all have to work together:

• A website fast enough and structured well enough to actually rank locally

• Clear, immediate access to emergency contact (WhatsApp lines have made a bigger difference here than almost anything else)

• Real patient reviews and before/after results displayed with credibility, not buried in a testimonials page nobody visits

• Local SEO fundamentals — Google Business Profile accuracy, consistent NAP (name, address, phone) data, and a sitemap that actually gets crawled
Get one of those wrong, and the rest doesn’t matter.
What “customer first” actually looks like in practice
It’s easy to say a business is “customer first.” It’s much harder to let that shape real technical decisions — especially ones that are more work.
For us, customer-first has meant things like:

• Auditing a client’s entire online presence before touching a single design element — not because it’s exciting work, but because a beautiful website sitting on broken technical SEO helps no one.

• Treating the patient’s journey as the actual product, not the clinic’s internal preferences. A gorgeous homepage means nothing if a scared, in-pain patient can’t find the emergency number in three seconds.

• Building bilingual (EN/NL) service pages because patients search in both languages, and forcing them to guess which one a clinic “prefers” is a needless barrier.

• Being honest with clients when the fix that will move the needle most isn’t the flashy one they asked for — it’s the boring one, like resolving a duplicate Google listing that’s been quietly splitting their reviews for years.
That last point might be the most important lesson from this whole journey: the most valuable thing you can offer a client isn’t always the service they came in asking for. Sometimes it’s telling them what’s actually broken.
The full-stack approach
Today, StepUp Analytics works across the full stack a small business actually needs to grow digitally — not just one slice of it:

• Web design & development — modern, fast, properly structured sites built for both patients and search engines, not just visual polish

• SEO — technical audits, local SEO, Google Business Profile optimization, and cleaning up the kind of legacy content and structural issues that quietly sabotage rankings

• Digital marketing — social content, local campaigns, and review/reputation strategies that bring the right patients to the door in the first place, not just a polished site waiting for traffic that never arrives

• Branding — because a trustworthy healthcare brand needs consistent visual and written identity across every touchpoint, not just a logo

• AI automation — the newest layer, helping practices automate the admin and communication work that pulls staff away from patient care
That last piece — AI automation — is where the data science roots and the healthcare focus have started to genuinely merge again. It turns out the instinct that started this whole journey (build things that are actually useful to the person on the other end, not just technically interesting) applies just as much to automation as it did to the first website audit.
What I’d tell someone starting an agency today
Don’t assume your first specialty is your final one. StepUp Analytics didn’t become a healthcare-focused digital agency because of a five-year plan — it became one because we kept showing up for the same kinds of problems, in the same kinds of businesses, and got good at solving them.
And if there’s one thing that’s stayed constant from the R-scripts era to now, it’s this: the technology is never the point. The person waiting on the other end of it — a patient trying to book an emergency appointment, a small business owner trying to be found — always is.

StepUp Analytics is an Amsterdam-based web development, SEO, digital marketing, branding, and AI automation agency, specializing in end-to-end digital transformation for healthcare practices including dental and physiotherapy clinics. Learn more at stepupanalytics.com.
